description: Tests CSRF tokens from meta tags are included in action requests and validated server-side

browserSteps.value: const csrfToken = document.querySelector('meta[name="csrf-token"]'); const csrfEph = document.querySelector('meta[name="csrf-ephemeral"]'); document.getElementById('csrf-check').textContent = 'token:' + (csrfToken ? csrfToken.content.length > 0 : false) + ' ephemeral:' + (csrfEph ? csrfEph.content.length > 0 : false); document.getElementById('csrf-check').style.display = 'block';
Click echo button - action should succeed with valid CSRF
Send raw fetch with tampered CSRF - should get 403

fetch('/_piko/actions/echo.Message', { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json', 'X-CSRF-Action-Token':'invalid-token'}, body: JSON.stringify({text:'hacked', _csrf_ephemeral_token:'invalid'}) }).then(r => { document.getElementById('csrf-status').textContent = String(r.status); document.getElementById('csrf-status').style.display = 'block'; return r.json(); }).then(d => { document.getElementById('csrf-error').textContent = d.error || 'none'; document.getElementById('csrf-error').style.display = 'block'; }).catch(e => { document.getElementById('csrf-error').textContent = 'fetch-error:' + e.message; document.getElementById('csrf-error').style.display = 'block'; });
